testcases.input: nums = [3,7,8,1,1,5], space = 2 Output: 1 Explanation: If we seed the machine with nums[3], then we destroy all targets equal to 1,3,5,7,9,... In this case, we would destroy 5 total targets (all except for nums[2]). It is impossible to destroy more than 5 targets, so we return nums[3].
testcases.expectedOutput: 1 Explanation: If we seed the machine with nums[3], then we destroy all targets equal to 1,3,5,7,9,... In this case, we would destroy 5 total targets (all except for nums[2]). It is impossible to destroy more than 5 targets, so we return nums[3].

testcases.input: nums = [1,3,5,2,4,6], space = 2 Output: 1 Explanation: Seeding the machine with nums[0], or nums[3] destroys 3 targets. It is not possible to destroy more than 3 targets. Since nums[0] is the minimal integer that can destroy 3 targets, we return 1.
testcases.expectedOutput: 1 Explanation: Seeding the machine with nums[0], or nums[3] destroys 3 targets. It is not possible to destroy more than 3 targets. Since nums[0] is the minimal integer that can destroy 3 targets, we return 1.

testcases.input: nums = [6,2,5], space = 100 Output: 2 Explanation: Whatever initial seed we select, we can only destroy 1 target. The minimal seed is nums[1].
testcases.expectedOutput: 2 Explanation: Whatever initial seed we select, we can only destroy 1 target. The minimal seed is nums[1].
